_comment: Medicine data read from the dispensary labels (Life St George's Hospital, patient Van Wyk, Marie-Jean) and verified against South African product info. Edit freely. 'cycle' = morning/evening, 'regular' = must take every time, otherwise PRN (only when in pain).

medicines.name: Axolta 10 mg
medicines.purpose: Blood thinner – prevents blood clots (DVT) in the legs after surgery.
medicines.instructions: Take 1 tablet ONCE daily WITH food. Complete the full course – do NOT skip a dose.
medicines.warnings: Most important medicine to not miss. Tell the doctor about any unusual bleeding or bruising.

medicines.name: Belanex 75 mg
medicines.purpose: Calms nerve pain after the knee surgery.
medicines.instructions: Take 1 capsule TWICE a day (every 12 hours).
medicines.warnings: May cause drowsiness/dizziness. Do not stop suddenly.

medicines.name: Colcibra 200 mg
medicines.purpose: Anti-inflammatory painkiller (NSAID) – reduces pain and swelling.
medicines.instructions: Take 1 capsule ONCE daily AFTER food when needed for pain and inflammation.
medicines.warnings: Always take with food to protect the stomach. Once daily only.

medicines.name: Xonoco XR 10/5 mg
medicines.instructions: Take 1 tablet every 12 hours AFTER food when needed for pain. Swallow whole – do not crush or chew.
medicines.warnings: Strong opioid. May cause drowsiness/constipation. Do not combine with extra codeine products without asking the doctor.

medicines.name: Dynapayne
medicines.active: Paracetamol 320 mg + Meprobamate 150 mg + Codeine 8 mg + Caffeine 32 mg
medicines.instructions: Take 1 to 2 tablets AFTER food when needed for pain. Do NOT exceed 8 tablets in 24 hours.
medicines.warnings: Contains paracetamol AND codeine – do not take other paracetamol/codeine products at the same time. May cause drowsiness. Max 8 in 24h.
